ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: By the fig and the olive
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: By the fig and the olive ˹of Jerusalem˺,

ayahs.textArabic: لَقَدۡ خَلَقۡنَا ٱلۡإِنسَٰنَ فِيٓ أَحۡسَنِ تَقۡوِيمٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Laqad khalaqna alinsana fee ahsani taqweemin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: We have certainly created man in the best of stature;
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, We created humans in the best form.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then We return him to the lowest of the low,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But We will reduce them to the lowest of the low ˹in Hell˺,

ayahs.textArabic: إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ وَعَمِلُواْ ٱلصَّٰلِحَٰتِ فَلَهُمۡ أَجۡرٌ غَيۡرُ مَمۡنُونٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Illa allatheena amanoo waAAamiloo alssalihati falahum ajrun ghayru mamnoonin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Except for those who believe and do righteous deeds, for they will have a reward uninterrupted.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: except those who believe and do good—they will have a never-ending reward.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So what yet causes you to deny the Recompense?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Now, what makes you deny the ˹final˺ Judgment?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Is not Allah the most just of judges?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Is Allah not the most just of all judges?
